basics.name: Sudhakar Reddy Peddinti
basics.label: Staff Software Engineer | Platforms & Search Systems
basics.summary: Staff Software Engineer with 10+ years building Java/Spring Boot microservices that power search and product-discovery platforms at a top-3 global retailer. Owned the full lifecycle of 7 production services — from API design and data-layer migration through deployment, observability, and on-call — across 28 repositories. Specialist in polyglot data architectures (PostgreSQL, Cassandra, CosmosDB, ElasticSearch), multi-tenant platform onboarding, and zero-downtime schema migrations. Consistently elevated service ownership from feature delivery to architecture strategy, cross-team contract definition, and platform-wide modernization.

work.name: Walmart
work.summary: Lead architectural strategy for a platform of 22+ backend services that control search ranking, faceted navigation, and merchandising rules for 10+ global markets. Own cross-team API contracts, multi-tenant onboarding roadmaps, and org-wide infrastructure modernization.
highlights: Architected zero-downtime multi-tenant onboarding for Item-Pinning service, merging regional codebases and implementing tenant-aware DAOs with dual-writes across AzureSQL and GCP PostgreSQL, successfully rolling out 5 global tenants.
Engineered Facet Lifecycle Management backend with state machine driven automated expiration, managing 82,000+ curations backed by CosmosDB and ElasticSearch.
Designed resilient v2 read path for Facet Sync pipeline with idempotent processing and Cassandra TTL contracts, eliminating stale-data race conditions and reducing legacy footprint.

Spearheaded infrastructure modernization across 22+ microservices, driving Spring Boot 3 migration, secret rotation (Akeyless), and Kubernetes probe standardization to resolve CVEs and improve platform security posture.

Developed a Python-based AI diagnostic tool (Sherlock) integrating multiple search-cache APIs to automate search-quality investigations, reducing debug time and establishing standard interaction mappings.

work.name: Walmart
work.summary: Took full technical ownership of the core rules-management backend (81K+ lines) and expanded scope to system design, data-model migrations, and experimentation infrastructure across 12 active repositories.
highlights: Designed and delivered the V2 rules engine, replacing legacy multi-table schemas with a versioned NoSQL model and executing a seamless 4-phase migration of 58K+ rules using feature flags and automated regression gates.
Built the Affinity Override Spring Boot service from the ground up, implementing generic DAOs to optimize data access and Azure Blob Storage integrations for scalable bulk data ingestion.

Architected A/B testing and interleaving experiment infrastructure for search-ranking rules, implementing combinatorial safeguards and event-driven notifications to validate search algorithms at scale.

Led zero-downtime database migrations from AzureSQL to GCP Cloud SQL for core services, establishing highly available cache clusters and standardizing the migration playbook across multiple platform teams.
Extracted the Category Intervention Dashboard from a monolithic architecture into an independent microservice, establishing the primary operations interface for 100+ domain specialists.
Led rapid Log4Shell vulnerability remediation across multiple tier-1 Java microservices within 48 hours, resolving complex Maven dependency conflicts.

work.name: Walmart
work.summary: Joined the Search Platform team and contributed across 4 repositories in year one — building core backend services for search-curation and item-ranking tooling from the ground up.
highlights: Developed foundational components of the Item-Pinning Service, resolving critical Couchbase connection bottlenecks and establishing initial multi-tenant configuration models to support global scale.
Engineered the Manual Shelves Syncer microservice to handle incremental and full shelf-data synchronization, and established comprehensive operational alerting runbooks.
Standardized CI/CD automation and event-driven job orchestration using Concord, establishing configuration patterns adopted across the entire platform service portfolio.
Delivered core REST APIs and data models for internal platforms, standardizing distributed logging strategies and creating foundational troubleshooting playbooks for on-call teams.
Initiated modular decoupling of specialist intervention workflows from legacy monoliths, designing extensible schemas that enabled future service extraction.

work.name: Accenture
highlights: Implemented database query optimizations reducing execution times to under 35 seconds.
Developed enhancements and bug fixes for high-impact applications using Java, Hibernate, and Spring MVC.
Led critical problem management and root cause analysis, earning the Best Performance award for production issue resolution.
Automated CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Maven to ensure stable patch deployments.

work.name: University of Missouri - Kansas City
highlights: Conducted data analytics and statistical processing to extract research insights.
Developed a React.js and Redux web dashboard for interactive data visualization.
Designed serverless backend APIs utilizing AWS S3, Lambda, and API Gateway.

skills.name: Observability & Reliability
keywords: CVE Remediation (Log4Shell, Postgres libs, CentOS EOL)

key_differentiators.summary: Why hire me as a Staff Platform Engineer

points.detail: I have managed 28 repositories over 8 years — not as a contributor, but as the primary owner. I have built 4 services from scratch, migrated 3 data layers (Couchbase→Cassandra, AzureSQL→PostgreSQL, legacy→CosmosDB), and maintained production SLAs throughout. I understand Java/Spring Boot at the level of Maven BOM conflicts, ThreadLocal connection-pool tuning, and log4j transitive dependency resolution.

Every migration I have delivered — Presto v1→v2 (58K rules), Couchbase→MeghaCache+Cassandra, AzureSQL→GCP PostgreSQL, 5-tenant WCP onboarding — used dual-write DAOs, CCM feature flags, and phased rollouts to guarantee zero downtime. I treat safe migration as a first-class engineering discipline, not an afterthought.

Beyond my owned services, I contribute to the shared library (cosmo-db-util), CI/CD pipeline configs, regression automation, Grafana/Prometheus dashboards, and runtime feature-flag configurations. I operate as a platform engineer across the full stack — from API contract to Kubernetes probe tuning to Kafka consumer-lag alerting.
